title: dalbergia kunstleri
intro: Dalbergia kunstleri, also known as the Borneo rosewood or keruing, is a type of tropical tree native to the island of Borneo. It is valued for its timber, which is used in furniture-making, but also holds medicinal properties, including its ability to reduce fever, alleviate pain, and act as an antibacterial agent. The health benefits of Dalbergia kunstleri include its potential to treat various ailments such as diarrhea, fever, and rheumatism, as well as its use in traditional medicine to support digestive health. Herbal preparations of Dalbergia kunstleri are typically made from the tree's bark or leaves and can be consumed as a tea or used in traditional remedies to support overall well-being.
